name: claude-night-market
description: Collection of 16 Claude Code plugins for software engineering workflows: git operations, code review, spec-driven development, issue management, session management, security controls, OAuth flows, and cross-session state management. Includes hooks for TDD enforcement, risk gating, and homeostatic skill stability monitoring.

findings_summary.notes: Extensive, feature-rich multi-plugin marketplace (MIT) with 16 plugins. Very high complexity — 72 open issues is a significant concern for stability. Plugins include: abstract, archetypes, attune, conjure, conserve, hookify, imbue, leyline, memory-palace, minister, parseltongue, pensive, sanctum, scribe, scry, spec-kit. Has pre-commit hooks, Makefile, pyproject.toml, and comprehensive documentation. Security concerns to note: (1) 'leyline' manages OAuth flows for GitHub/GitLab/AWS with local token caching — this involves credential handling which requires careful trust evaluation. (2) 'conserve' implements permission checks that block/approve commands — this is a powerful hook that could block legitimate operations if misconfigured. (3) The system integrates deeply with GitHub Discussions for cross-session state. (4) Complexity is very high — README is extremely long and references many versions of Claude Code. The hooks operate at a privileged level (PreToolUse blocking). The conserve plugin's 'automatically approving safe commands like ls while blocking high-risk operations like rm -rf /' description is security-relevant. No obvious malicious patterns, but high complexity and 72 open issues warrant cautious deployment.
